text: The patient is positioned in the right decubitus position, and the surgeon makes an intercostal uniportal incision at the fifth intercostal space on the left side. EndoGIS tablets are used to perform resections of the two lesions in the left lower lobe.

The surgeon inserts a chest drain through the existing port after confirming atypical adenomatous hyperplasia in both lesions via frozen section pathology. The surgical wound is then closed in layers.

The patient is positioned in the left decubitus position with the chest drain secured between pillows. A three-centimeter incision is made in the right fifth intercostal space. The surgeon begins resection of the superior segment of the right lower lobe by dissecting and opening the fissure above the pulmonary artery, followed by stapling the fissure using an energy stapler.

The surgeon dissects, encircles, and divides the segmental bronchus to segment six using an endo GIS device. The venous branch to segment six is dissected, clipped, and divided with an energy device. Two small arterial branches supplying segment six are identified, dissected, clipped, and divided using the energy device.
Two endogyrary loads are used to complete the resection, and the segment is visibly removed from the chest cavity. The final pathology of the ground-glass opacity (GGO) reveals carcinoma in situ.
